titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/30

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=286 status=live modified=2021-04-06 10:00:49.643302+00 | translation moa=287 status=live modified=2021-04-06 10:00:45.739767+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte matris Ecclesie filiis tam presentibus quam futuris . Dunecanus filius Gileberti filii fergus . Salutem . Sciatis me dedisse et concessisse et hac mea carta confirmasse deo et Ecclesie
sancte marie de Melros . et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus pro animabus Dauid . et Melcolmi Regum dominorum meorum . et Comitis . Henrici . et pro salute domini mei Willelmi Regis . et Dauid fratris eius .
et Comitis . Dunecani . et pro salute mea . et animabus patris mei et matris mee . et omnium antecessorum et successorum meorum in liberam et puram et perpetuam elemosinam . totam terram de
Meibothelbeg . ut eam habeant et possideant in perpetuum liberam quietam et solutam ab omni terreno seruitio . et exactione et consuetudine seculari . et sicut aliqua elemosina liberius . quietius
plenius et honorificentius . possidetur in Regno Scott’ . per diuisas in hac mea carta nominatas . Scilicet a capite burne que descendit de Crubder . et per eandem burnam usque in Culelung
ford . et sicut eadem burna descendit de Culelungford in polnetyber . et sicut polnetyber cadit in Don . et per Don sursum usque ubi polgarroh cadit in don . et sic sursum per Polgarroh  usque ad paruam mussam . Notandum autem quod in parte australi eiusdem musse descendit sicus quidam in polgarroh . et per eundem sicum sursum usque diuidere sicus ascendit in Polcogri . et polcogri
adcendit in Dufah . inter Meibothelbeg . et Meibothelmor . et de eodem Dufah per sicum quendam qui ascendit in aliam mussam . et a capite sici illius per transuersum ipsius musse . et a mussa illa
sicut alius sicus in parte australi de Brokelaue ascendit in uiam . et per eandem uiam sursum usque ad nemorosam uallem que ascendit de predicto Crubder et per eandem uallem usque ubi eadem uallis
des[-i]cendit in Crubder . Preterea dedi eis et hac mea carta confirmaui totam terram de Bethoc . in liberam puram et perpetuam elemosinam per terminos et rectas diuisas suas .
Scilicet sicut uia uadit ad Scipsate . et per eandem uiam usque ad sicum qui uadit per mediam mussam usque ad capud de Altercreue . et inde usque ad uiam qua itur . ultra altecreue . et de illa
uia usque ad sicum qui ascendendo uadit ex aquilonali parte de Cragan . et per eundem sicum usque ad illum locum qui uocatur Tunregaid . ubi de ipso sico egreditur quidam Riuulus que dicitur polsalach
aric . et inde sicut idem Riuulus currit usque in Polnetyhered Totam hanc terram tam illam de Meitbothelbeg . quam istam de Bethoc . et quiquid continetur infra diuisas prenominatas in bosco
et plano . in pratis et pascuis . in uiis et semitis . in terris et aquis et piscariis . et in omnibus aliis rebus et aisiamentis dedi predictis monachis et Ecclesie de Melros . ut ea habeant et possi
deant in perpetuum libere . quiete plenarie honorifice et absolute ab omni terreno seruitio . sicut aliquam elemosinam liberius quietius plenius et honorificentius tenent et possident .
ita tamen quod singulis annis dabunt mihi et heredibus meis . ii . marcas . argenti . tantum ad recognitionem pro eadem terra de Bethoc . i . marcam . ad pentecosten . et alteram ad festum sancti martini . Et ipsi
monachi utentur omnibus que continentur infra prenominatas diuisas modis omnibus ad libitum suum melius et utilius sibi uisum fuerit . et ego et heredes mei manutenebimus et warantizabimus
eis hec omnia contra omnes homines in perpetuum . et adquietabimus uersus dominum Regem de omni forensi et terreno seruitio . Et quia uolui ut hec mea elemosina firma esset et stabilis et nullo modo in
irritum duci posset . horum omnium testem et fideiussorem posui Episcopum et Ecclesiam Glasguensem . ut si aliquando ego uel heredes mei a tenore huius carte deuiare temptauimus . ipsa Ecclesia et eius pon
tifices per Ecclesiasciam censuram ad correctionem nos reuocent . His Testibus . Domino Iocelino . Glasguensi . Episcopo . Willelmo abbate de Sancta Cruce . Comite . Dunnecano . Comite . Gileberto . Sim’ archidiacono . Domino Ro
lando . Melcolmo filio Comitis Dunecani . Eth filio Comitis . de Leuen’ . Willelmo Walter . Iohanne clericis Episcopi . Rog’ de Scaleb’ . Gilasald . mac Giland’ . Gillenem . mac Colem’ . Edg’ . camerario meo .

To all sons of Holy Mother Church as well present as future, Duncan son of Gilbert son of Fergus, Greeting: Know that I have given and granted and by this my charter made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melros and to the monks serving God there, for the souls of my lords, the kings David and Malcolm, and of Earl Henry, and for the salvation of my lord King William, and David his brother, and of Earl Duncan, and for my own salvation, and the souls of my father and my mother, and of all my ancestors and successors, in free and pure and perpetual alms, the whole land of Maybole-beg, as they have and possess it in perpetuity, free and quit and loosed from every earthly service and exaction and secular custom, and just as any alms is freely, quietly, fully and honourably possessed in the Kingdom of the Scots, by the boundaries named in this my charter. That is, from the head of the burn that descends from Crumder, and by the same burn as far as Culelungford, and as the same burn descends from Culelungford to Polnetyber, and as Polnetyber falls into the Doon, and by the Doon, upwards as far as where the Polgarroh falls into the Doon, and so upwards by Polgarroh as far as the small peat bog – it is to be noted, however, that on the southern side of the same peat bog, a syke descends into the Polgarroh – and by the same syke upwards as far as the syke goes up to divide into the Polcogri, and the Polcogri goes up to the Dufah, between Maybole-beg and Maybole-mór, and from the same Dufah by a syke which goes up to another peat bog, and from the head of that syke across that peat bog, and by the bog as the other syke on the south side of Brokelaue goes up to the road, and by the same road upwards as far as the wooded valley which ascends from the said Crubder and by the same valley as far as where the same valley descends to Crubder. In addition, I have given to them and by this my charter have made firm the whole land of Beoch, in free, pure and perpetual alms, by their bounds and right marches. That is, as the road goes to Scipsate, and by the same road as far as the syke that goes through the middle of the bog as far as the head of Altecreue, and thence as far as the road that goes beyond Altecreue and from that road as far as the syke that by ascending proceeds from the north side of Cragan, and by the same syke as far as that place which is called Tunregaid, where from that syke a stream which is called Polsalacharic comes forth, and thence as the same stream runs, as far as to Polnetyhered. This whole land as well that of Maybole-beg as that of Beoch, and whatever is contained within the aforenamed bounds in wood and plain, in meadows and pastures, in roads and paths, in lands and waters and fisheries, and in all other things and easements I have given to the said monks and the church of Melrose, that they may have and possess them in perpetuity, freely, quietly, fully, honourably, and loosed from every earthly service, just as they hold and possess any alms freely, quietly, fully and honourably, provided that they shall give to me and my heirs, each year, 2 merks of silver only, for recognition for the same land of Beoch, 1 merk at Pentecost, and the other at the feast of St Martin. And the monks shall use everything that is contained within the aforenamed boundaries at their pleasure by all means that seem good and useful to them. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant all these things to them against all men in perpetuity, and we shall acquit with respect to the lord king for all foreign and tenant service. And since I have wished that this my alms should be firm and established and by no means could be brought to nought, I have placed the bishop and the church of Glasgow as witness and security for all these things, so that if at any time I or my heirs are tempted to deviate from the terms of this charter, the church and its bishops may recall us to correction by ecclesiastical censure. With these witnesses, the lord Jocelin, bishop of Glasgow, William abbot of Holyrood, Earl Duncan, Earl Gilbert, Simon the archdeacon, the lord Roland, Malcolm the son of Earl Duncan, Aed son of the earl of Lennox, William, Walter and John, the bishop’s clerks, Roger of Skelbrooke, Gilla Asaild mac Gillanders, Gilla na Náem mac Colmán, Edgar my chamberlain.
